user_story: As an infrastructure operator, I want to launch a one-off discovery scan for a provider so that I can probe a network without first creating a reusable definition.
expected_behavior: Maps the body 'provider' (nmap, ssh, active-directory, snmp) to a BullMQ queue, generates a uuid job id, and enqueues a DiscoveryJob (method 'agentless', status 'pending') with 3 attempts and exponential backoff. Returns 201 with the job id/provider/status. Unknown or missing provider returns 400; queue errors return 500. NOTE: cloud/API providers (AWS/Azure/GCP/K8s) are intentionally excluded here and handled by Connectors.
issue_note: scheduleDiscoverySchema (discovery.routes.ts:23-31) declares keys '_provider'/'_config' but the controller reads req.body.provider/config (discovery.controller.ts:49); Joi validate uses stripUnknown:true (common/utils/validators.ts:36), so the real 'provider' is stripped and required '_provider' is always missing -> every request fails validation with 400.

user_story: As an operator, I want to check the live status of a scheduled discovery job so that I know whether it is pending, running, completed, or failed.
expected_behavior: Searches all four discovery queues (nmap, ssh, active-directory, snmp) for the job id, returning the first match. Responds 200 with sanitized job data (credentials and secret fields redacted), BullMQ state, progress, returnvalue, failedReason, queue name, attemptsMade and timestamps. Missing id returns 400; a job not found in any queue returns 404; errors return 500.

feature: List ad-hoc discovery jobs with filters/pagination
user_story: As an operator, I want to list and filter discovery jobs by status and provider so that I can review recent and in-flight scans.
expected_behavior: Aggregates jobs across the four discovery queues, optionally filtering by status (pending/running/completed/failed) and provider, sanitizes each job (redacted secrets), sorts by created_at descending, and applies limit/offset pagination. Returns 200 with the job array plus a pagination block (total/count/offset/limit). Errors return 500.
issue_note: listJobsQuerySchema (discovery.routes.ts:33-38) uses keys '_status'/'_provider'/'_limit'/'_offset' while listJobs reads req.query.status/provider/limit/offset (discovery.controller.ts:220); validateOptional + stripUnknown discards the caller's real query params, so status/provider filtering and pagination are silently ignored whenever any query string is supplied.

user_story: As an operator, I want to cancel a pending or running discovery job so that I can stop an unwanted or misconfigured scan.
expected_behavior: Locates the job across the four discovery queues and removes it via job.remove(). Returns 200 with the id and previousState. Missing id returns 400; job not found returns 404; jobs already in 'completed' or 'failed' state cannot be cancelled and return 400; errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to create a reusable discovery definition bundling provider, method, credential and schedule so that scans can be re-run consistently.
expected_behavior: Validates name/provider/method (credential_id required unless provider is nmap; agent_id required when method is 'agent') and delegates to DiscoveryDefinitionService.createDefinition with created_by taken from req.user?.id or 'system'. Returns 201 with the created definition. Credential validation failures (message contains 'Credential') return 400; other failures return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to list discovery definitions filtered by provider, active state, or creator so that I can manage saved scan configurations.
expected_behavior: Builds a filter object from optional query params provider, is_active ('true'/'false' string coerced to boolean 'active'), and created_by, then returns 200 with the matching definitions array and a count. Errors return 500. Query validation accepts only the documented keys.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to retrieve a single discovery definition so that I can inspect its full configuration.
expected_behavior: Fetches the definition via the service. Returns 200 with the definition on success. Missing id returns 400; a non-existent definition returns 404; errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to update a discovery definition's settings so that I can adjust provider, credential, schedule, or activation without recreating it.
expected_behavior: Validates the partial update body (all fields optional) and applies it through the service. Returns 200 with the updated definition. Missing id returns 400; service errors whose message contains 'not found' return 404; 'Credential' errors return 400; other errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to delete a discovery definition so that obsolete scan configurations are removed.
expected_behavior: Deletes the definition via the service and returns 204 No Content with an empty body on success. Missing id returns 400; a 'not found' error returns 404; other errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger an immediate run of a saved discovery definition so that I can scan now without waiting for the schedule.
expected_behavior: Calls service.runDefinition(id, triggeredBy) where triggeredBy is req.user?.id or 'system', queues the discovery job, then re-fetches the definition for its provider. Returns 202 Accepted with job_id, definition_id, provider, and status 'pending'. Missing id returns 400; 'not found' returns 404; other errors return 500.

feature: Enable scheduled execution for a definition
user_story: As an administrator, I want to enable a definition's cron schedule so that discovery runs automatically on a recurring basis.
expected_behavior: Calls service.enableSchedule(id) and returns 200 with the updated definition. Missing id returns 400; a 'not found' error returns 404; an error indicating 'no schedule configured' returns 400 (a cron expression must already exist on the definition); other errors return 500.

feature: Disable scheduled execution for a definition
user_story: As an administrator, I want to disable a definition's recurring schedule so that automatic discovery runs stop.
expected_behavior: Calls service.disableSchedule(id) and returns 200 with the updated definition. Missing id returns 400; a 'not found' error returns 404; other errors return 500.

feature: Register or update a discovery agent
user_story: As a remote discovery agent, I want to register myself with the CMDB so that the platform knows my capabilities and reachable networks for job assignment.
expected_behavior: Validates agent_id, hostname, non-empty provider_capabilities, and non-empty reachable_networks (each a valid IPv4 CIDR), plus optional version/platform/arch/api_endpoint(uri)/tags. Delegates to DiscoveryAgentService.registerAgent (upsert semantics) and returns 200 with the persisted agent. Validation failures return 400 via middleware; service errors return 500.

user_story: As a discovery agent, I want to send periodic heartbeats with my status and stats so that the platform treats me as alive and tracks my throughput.
expected_behavior: Validates agent_id and status (active/inactive/offline/disabled) plus optional stats (jobs_completed/jobs_failed/cis_discovered). Calls service.updateHeartbeat(body) and returns 200 with message 'Heartbeat updated'. Validation failures return 400; service errors return 500.

feature: Find best agent for target networks
user_story: As the discovery orchestrator, I want to find the best-suited active agent that can reach a set of target networks for a provider so that jobs route to a capable agent.
expected_behavior: Validates targetNetworks (non-empty IPv4 CIDR array) and provider, then calls service.findBestAgentForNetworks. Returns 200 with { agent_id } when a match is found. Missing/empty targetNetworks or missing provider return 400 (also defended in-controller); when no active agent can reach the networks it returns 404; other errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to list registered agents filtered by status, provider, or tags so that I can monitor my agent fleet.
expected_behavior: Reads optional query params status, provider, and tags (single value coerced to array) into a filter object and returns 200 with the agents array and a count. No required validation. Service errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to retrieve a single agent by id so that I can inspect its capabilities, networks, and last status.
expected_behavior: Fetches the agent via service.getAgent(agentId). Returns 200 with the agent. Missing agentId returns 400; a non-existent agent returns 404 ('Agent not found'); errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to delete an agent registration so that decommissioned agents are removed from assignment.
expected_behavior: Calls service.deleteAgent(agentId) and returns 200 with message 'Agent deleted successfully'. Missing agentId returns 400; errors return 500. (Returns 200 rather than 204 and does not pre-check existence.)

user_story: As an operator, I want overall job statistics for every discovery and ETL queue so that I can gauge system load at a glance.
expected_behavior: Iterates a fixed list of 8 queues (discovery:nmap/ssh/active-directory/snmp and etl:sync/full-refresh/change-detection/reconciliation), calling queueManager.getQueueStats for each; a per-queue failure yields a stats object with an error field instead of aborting. Returns 200 with queues stats and totalQueues. Top-level errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to view configured recurring discovery schedules so that I can see what runs automatically.
expected_behavior: Currently a stub: getSchedules() is not implemented on the DiscoveryOrchestrator, so the handler returns 200 with an empty data array and message 'No scheduled discovery jobs configured' to avoid breaking the UI. Unexpected errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to view ETL schedules so that I understand recurring data-processing jobs.
expected_behavior: Not implemented: returns 501 Not Implemented with message 'ETL schedule management not yet implemented' advising callers to use BullMQ repeatable jobs directly. Unexpected errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to change the cron pattern for a provider's recurring discovery so that scans run at the cadence I choose.
expected_behavior: Reads provider param and cronPattern body and logs the request, but updateSchedule() is not yet implemented on the DiscoveryOrchestrator, so it returns 501 Not Implemented ('Discovery schedule update not yet implemented'). Unexpected errors return 500.

user_story: As an administrator, I want to change an ETL job type's schedule so that data syncs run on my preferred cadence.
expected_behavior: Not implemented: returns 501 Not Implemented with message 'ETL schedule management not yet implemented' (echoing the type), advising use of BullMQ repeatable jobs. Unexpected errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want per-provider discovery queue stats enriched with the number of CIs discovered so that I can measure discovery effectiveness.
expected_behavior: For each of the four discovery queues, fetches queue stats and queries Neo4j for the count of active/inactive/maintenance CIs whose discovery_provider matches (checking both the top-level field and a metadata substring for backward compatibility). Returns 200 with per-provider stats and totalDiscoveredCIs. A queue failure yields a zeroed fallback object with an error note; Neo4j failures default the CI count to 0; top-level errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want a paginated, provider/status-filterable list of discovery jobs so that I can review discovery activity at scale.
expected_behavior: Reads page/limit/provider/status query params; scopes to one queue when provider is given, else all four. Pulls job ids directly from Redis bull sorted sets (completed/failed/active/waiting/delayed depending on status), loads each via Job.fromId to obtain full fields including returnvalue, maps to a normalized shape, sorts by createdAt desc, and paginates. Returns 200 with data and a pagination block (page/limit/total/totalPages). Per-queue/per-job failures are tolerated; top-level errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger an immediate discovery run for a provider through the scheduler so that a scan starts now with my supplied config.
expected_behavior: Reads provider param and { config, triggeredBy } body, calls discoveryScheduler.triggerDiscovery(provider, config, triggeredBy|x-user-id header|'api'), and returns 202 Accepted with jobId, provider, and status 'queued'. Errors return 500. (No Joi validation on this route; provider is taken verbatim from the path.)

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger an ETL job by type so that I can kick off a data sync or reconciliation on demand.
expected_behavior: Not implemented: returns 501 Not Implemented ('ETL job triggering not yet implemented') with the type echoed, advising callers to enqueue ETL jobs via BullMQ directly. Unexpected errors return 500.

feature: List failed jobs for a queue
user_story: As an operator, I want to inspect failed jobs in a queue with their reasons and stack traces so that I can diagnose failures.
expected_behavior: Reads start/end range query params (default 0..99) and calls queueManager.getFailedJobs(queueName, start, end), mapping each to id/name/data/failedReason/stacktrace/attemptsMade/timestamps. Returns 200 with failedJobs and count. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

feature: Clean old jobs from a queue
user_story: As an operator, I want to purge old completed or failed jobs from a queue so that Redis storage stays bounded.
expected_behavior: Reads body grace (default 3600000ms), limit (default 1000), and type (default 'completed', also 'failed') and calls queueManager.cleanQueue with them. Returns 200 confirming the cleaned type and grace window. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

feature: Get job status in a named queue
user_story: As an operator, I want the full status of a specific job in a specific queue so that I can track its progress and result.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.getJob(queueName, jobId) and returns 200 with id, name, queueName, state, progress, data, returnvalue, failedReason, attemptsMade, and timestamps. Missing queueName or jobId returns 400; a job not present in the queue returns 404; errors return 500. (Registered after the more specific /failed route to avoid route shadowing.)

feature: List jobs in a named queue by state
user_story: As an operator, I want to page through jobs in a queue filtered by lifecycle state so that I can monitor a specific queue.
expected_behavior: Reads state (default 'waiting'; also active/completed/failed/delayed), start (0) and end (99) query params and fetches the corresponding BullMQ list, mapping each job to id/name/state/data/progress/attemptsMade/timestamps. Returns 200 with queueName, state, jobs and count. Unknown state falls back to waiting. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

feature: Cancel/remove a job in a named queue
user_story: As an operator, I want to remove a specific job from a queue so that I can clear a stuck or unwanted job.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.removeJob(queueName, jobId), logs the cancellation, and returns 200 with jobId, queueName, and a success message. Missing queueName or jobId returns 400; errors (including job-not-found surfaced by the manager) return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to retry a failed job so that I can recover from transient errors without recreating the job.
expected_behavior: Calls queueManager.retryJob(queueName, jobId), logs the retry, and returns 200 with jobId, queueName, and a success message. Missing queueName or jobId returns 400; errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want statistics for every configured queue plus rolled-up totals so that I can monitor the whole queue system.
expected_behavior: Iterates Object.values(QUEUE_NAMES); for each, fetches getQueueStats plus isPaused and getJobCounts. Computes aggregate totals (waiting/active/completed/failed/delayed) across queues. Returns 200 with per-queue stats and the aggregate block. Per-queue failures yield an error entry; top-level errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want detailed stats for one queue including repeatable jobs and recent activity so that I can deep-dive a specific queue.
expected_behavior: Fetches getQueueStats, isPaused, getJobCounts, getRepeatableJobs, plus the 10 most recent completed and failed jobs. Returns 200 with stats, pause state, counts, repeatable-job descriptors (key/name/pattern/next), and trimmed recent completed/failed job summaries. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

user_story: As an SRE, I want latency and throughput metrics for a queue so that I can spot performance regressions.
expected_behavior: Loads up to 100 completed jobs, derives per-job latency (finishedOn - processedOn) and computes avg/min/max in ms, and estimates throughput as jobs/min from jobs finished in the last hour divided by 60. Returns 200 with latency, throughput, completedJobs count, and a '1 hour' time window. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

user_story: As an SRE, I want the running status of all discovery and ETL workers so that I can confirm processing capacity is healthy.
expected_behavior: Fetches statuses from the discovery worker manager (an array) and the ETL worker manager (a health object), derives discoveryHealthy from every worker's _isRunning (or the object's healthy field) and etlHealthy from its healthy field, and returns 200 with { discovery, etl, overallHealthy }. Errors return 500.

user_story: As an SRE, I want an aggregated health verdict across all queues so that I can drive alerting and dashboards.
expected_behavior: For each queue derives a status: 'warning' if paused or waiting>1000, 'degraded' if failed>100, 'unhealthy' on error, else 'healthy', collecting human-readable issues and job counts. Rolls up to an overallStatus (unhealthy if any unhealthy, else degraded if any degraded, else healthy) and a summary tally. Returns 200; top-level errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to pause a queue so that I can halt new job processing during maintenance.
expected_behavior: Resolves the queue and calls queue.pause(), logs it, and returns 200 with queueName and a success message. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

user_story: As an operator, I want to resume a paused queue so that job processing continues after maintenance.
expected_behavior: Resolves the queue and calls queue.resume(), logs it, and returns 200 with queueName and a success message. Missing queueName returns 400; errors return 500.

feature: Get logs for a specific job
user_story: As an operator, I want to read the execution logs of a specific job so that I can troubleshoot what happened during its run.
expected_behavior: Fetches the job via queueManager.getJob and is intended to return its accumulated log lines. Missing queueName/jobId returns 400; a job not found returns 404; errors return 500. Intended success response is 200 with jobId, queueName, and logs.
issue_note: getJobLogs reads logs with 'const logs = await job.log' (queue.controller.ts:448), but in BullMQ job.log is the append-a-log-line method, not a logs accessor (logs are retrieved via queue.getJobLogs(jobId)); awaiting the method reference yields the function/undefined, so the response 'logs' field never contains the actual job logs.
